mitre_attack: T1530 - Data from Cloud Storage Object
cwe: CWE-732 - Incorrect Permission Assignment for Critical Resource

threat.title: GCP Cloud Storage IAM Misconfiguration - Public Bucket Exposure
threat.description: When Google Cloud Storage (GCS) buckets are misconfigured with 'allUsers' or 'allAuthenticatedUsers' IAM bindings, attackers can enumerate and exfiltrate sensitive data without authentication. This vulnerability is one of the most critical cloud misconfigurations, responsible for numerous high-profile data breaches affecting millions of users. Unlike traditional access controls, these IAM bindings grant broad permissions that bypass organization boundaries and VPC Service Controls.

attack.scenario:
1. Attacker uses automated bucket enumeration tools (gcpbucketbrute, GrayhatWarfare) to discover GCS buckets
2. Discovers bucket with 'allUsers' or 'allAuthenticatedUsers' IAM binding via public bucket listing
3. Enumerates bucket contents using gsutil or GCS API without authentication
4. Downloads sensitive files including databases, backups, API keys, PII, or proprietary data
5. May upload malicious content to publicly writable buckets (if Storage Object Creator role granted)
6. Exfiltrates data for ransom, competitive advantage, or sells on dark web markets
prerequisites: GCS bucket has 'allUsers' or 'allAuthenticatedUsers' IAM member binding
Bucket name is discoverable through enumeration or leaked in code/DNS records
Uniform bucket-level access not enforced with restrictive Organization Policies
VPC Service Controls not implemented or improperly configured
Bucket lacks signed URL expiration or validation

cve_references.description: Spotify exposed 300GB of user data via publicly accessible GCS bucket containing login credentials and internal API keys
Lion Air exposed 21 million passenger records including passports, flight bookings, and payment data via misconfigured GCS bucket

real_world_breaches.impact: 300GB of data including login credentials, internal API keys, and Spotify partner credentials exposed; affected internal systems and third-party integrations
real_world_breaches.root_cause: GCS bucket misconfigured with 'allUsers' storage.objects.viewer role, allowing public read access to sensitive backup files

real_world_breaches.impact: 21 million passenger records exposed including full names, phone numbers, passport details, flight bookings, and partial payment information
real_world_breaches.root_cause: Production GCS bucket with 'allAuthenticatedUsers' IAM binding allowed any Google account holder to access passenger manifests and booking databases

real_world_breaches.incident: Pokemon GO Player Location Data Exposure
real_world_breaches.impact: Exposed real-time location data, email addresses, and gameplay statistics for millions of players via misconfigured Firebase/GCS buckets
real_world_breaches.root_cause: Firebase Storage (GCS backend) configured with default permissive rules allowing 'allUsers' read access to user-generated content buckets

real_world_breaches.impact: Four publicly exposed GCS buckets containing 137GB of sensitive data including passwords, API keys, decryption keys, and proprietary client information
real_world_breaches.root_cause: Multiple production buckets configured with 'allUsers' storage.objects.list and storage.objects.get permissions without encryption or monitoring

bug_bounty_reports.summary: Public GCS bucket exposing customer PII and session tokens via 'allAuthenticatedUsers' IAM binding
Backup bucket with 'allUsers' viewer role exposing production database dumps and encryption keys

mitigations.title: Enforce Uniform Bucket-Level Access with Organization Policy Constraints
mitigations.description: Implement Organization Policy constraints to prevent 'allUsers' and 'allAuthenticatedUsers' IAM bindings at scale. Uniform bucket-level access disables ACLs and enforces IAM-only permissions, preventing mixed permission models that lead to misconfigurations.

tools.name: VPC Service Controls
tools.cost: $0.04 per GB data egress within perimeter

nist_csf: PR.PT-4 - Communications and control networks are protected
validation_steps: Verify Organization Policy constraints are applied: gcloud resource-manager org-policies list --organization=ORG_ID
Attempt to create bucket with 'allUsers' binding (should fail)
Verify uniform bucket-level access: gsutil uniformbucketlevelaccess get gs://BUCKET_NAME
Test VPC Service Controls by attempting access from outside perimeter
Review Cloud Asset Inventory for any public buckets: gcloud asset search-all-iam-policies --scope=organizations/ORG_ID --query='policy:allUsers OR policy:allAuthenticatedUsers'

mitigations.title: Implement Signed URLs with Short Expiration for External Access
mitigations.description: Instead of public IAM bindings, use time-limited signed URLs that grant temporary access to specific objects. This provides fine-grained control with automatic expiration and audit logging.

tools.name: Cloud KMS
tools.cost: $0.06 per key per month + $0.03 per 10,000 operations

nist_csf: DE.AE-3 - Event data are aggregated and correlated
validation_steps: Generate signed URL and verify it works only before expiration
Verify URL becomes invalid after expiration time
Test that URL cannot be modified without invalidating signature
Confirm signed URL access is logged in Cloud Logging
Verify service account has minimal IAM permissions (storage.objects.get only)

mitigations.title: Deploy Automated Bucket Scanning with Security Command Center
mitigations.description: Enable GCP Security Command Center and Cloud Asset Inventory to continuously monitor for public bucket misconfigurations and automatically remediate violations.

code_example.code:
#!/usr/bin/env python3
"""
Automated GCS Bucket Security Scanner and Remediation
Uses Security Command Center and Cloud Asset Inventory APIs
"""

from google.cloud import securitycenter_v1
from google.cloud import asset_v1
from google.cloud import storage
import logging
import os

logging.basicConfig(level=logging.INFO)
logger = logging.getLogger(__name__)

# Initialize clients
scc_client = securitycenter_v1.SecurityCenterClient()
asset_client = asset_v1.AssetServiceClient()
storage_client = storage.Client()

ORG_ID = os.environ['GCP_ORG_ID']
ORG_NAME = f"organizations/{ORG_ID}"

def scan_public_buckets():
    """
    Scan all GCS buckets in organization for public access
    Returns list of publicly accessible buckets
    """
    logger.info(f"Scanning organization {ORG_ID} for public buckets...")
    
    # Query Cloud Asset Inventory for bucket IAM policies
    request = asset_v1.SearchAllIamPoliciesRequest(
        scope=ORG_NAME,
        query='resource:storage.googleapis.com/Bucket AND (policy:allUsers OR policy:allAuthenticatedUsers)'
    )
    
    public_buckets = []
    
    try:
        page_result = asset_client.search_all_iam_policies(request=request)
        
        for policy in page_result:
            bucket_name = policy.resource.split('/')[-1]
            
            # Extract IAM bindings with public members
            public_bindings = []
            for binding in policy.policy.bindings:
                public_members = [m for m in binding.members 
                                if m in ['allUsers', 'allAuthenticatedUsers']]
                if public_members:
                    public_bindings.append({
                        'role': binding.role,
                        'members': public_members
                    })
            
            if public_bindings:
                public_buckets.append({
                    'bucket_name': bucket_name,
                    'resource': policy.resource,
                    'project': policy.project,
                    'public_bindings': public_bindings
                })
                
                logger.warning(f"Found public bucket: {bucket_name} with bindings: {public_bindings}")
        
        logger.info(f"Scan complete. Found {len(public_buckets)} public buckets")
        return public_buckets
        
    except Exception as e:
        logger.error(f"Error scanning buckets: {e}")
        raise

def create_scc_finding(bucket_info):
    """
    Create Security Command Center finding for public bucket
    """
    source_name = f"{ORG_NAME}/sources/-"
    finding_id = f"public-bucket-{bucket_info['bucket_name']}"
    
    finding = securitycenter_v1.Finding(
        state=securitycenter_v1.Finding.State.ACTIVE,
        category="PUBLIC_BUCKET_EXPOSURE",
        severity=securitycenter_v1.Finding.Severity.CRITICAL,
        resource_name=bucket_info['resource'],
        description=f"GCS bucket '{bucket_info['bucket_name']}' has public IAM bindings: {bucket_info['public_bindings']}",
        event_time={"seconds": int(time.time())},
        source_properties={
            "bucket_name": bucket_info['bucket_name'],
            "public_bindings": str(bucket_info['public_bindings']),
            "remediation": "Remove allUsers and allAuthenticatedUsers IAM members",
            "compliance_violation": "ISO27001 A.9.4.1, NIST CSF PR.AC-4"
        }
    )
    
    try:
        created_finding = scc_client.create_finding(
            request={
                "parent": source_name,
                "finding_id": finding_id,
                "finding": finding
            }
        )
        logger.info(f"Created SCC finding: {created_finding.name}")
        return created_finding
    except Exception as e:
        logger.error(f"Error creating SCC finding: {e}")
        return None

def remediate_public_bucket(bucket_info, auto_remediate=False):
    """
    Remove public IAM bindings from bucket
    
    Args:
        bucket_info: Dictionary with bucket details
        auto_remediate: If True, automatically remove public bindings
    """
    bucket_name = bucket_info['bucket_name']
    bucket = storage_client.bucket(bucket_name)
    
    try:
        # Get current IAM policy
        policy = bucket.get_iam_policy(requested_policy_version=3)
        
        # Remove public members
        modified = False
        for binding in policy.bindings:
            original_members = set(binding['members'])
            binding['members'] = [
                m for m in binding['members'] 
                if m not in ['allUsers', 'allAuthenticatedUsers']
            ]
            
            if len(binding['members']) < len(original_members):
                modified = True
                removed = original_members - set(binding['members'])
                logger.info(f"Removed {removed} from role {binding['role']}")
        
        if modified and auto_remediate:
            # Apply updated policy
            bucket.set_iam_policy(policy)
            logger.info(f"Successfully remediated bucket: {bucket_name}")
            
            # Update SCC finding to INACTIVE
            finding_name = f"{ORG_NAME}/sources/-/findings/public-bucket-{bucket_name}"
            scc_client.set_finding_state(
                request={
                    "name": finding_name,
                    "state": securitycenter_v1.Finding.State.INACTIVE
                }
            )
            return True
        elif modified:
            logger.info(f"Bucket {bucket_name} requires remediation (auto-remediate disabled)")
            return False
        else:
            logger.info(f"No public bindings found in current policy for {bucket_name}")
            return True
            
    except Exception as e:
        logger.error(f"Error remediating bucket {bucket_name}: {e}")
        return False

def enable_bucket_security_features(bucket_name):
    """
    Enable security best practices on bucket
    """
    bucket = storage_client.bucket(bucket_name)
    
    try:
        # Enable uniform bucket-level access
        bucket.iam_configuration.uniform_bucket_level_access_enabled = True
        bucket.iam_configuration.public_access_prevention = 'enforced'
        
        # Enable versioning
        bucket.versioning_enabled = True
        
        # Update bucket
        bucket.patch()
        
        logger.info(f"Enabled security features on bucket: {bucket_name}")
        return True
        
    except Exception as e:
        logger.error(f"Error enabling security features: {e}")
        return False

def main():
    """
    Main execution flow
    """
    logger.info("Starting GCS security scanner...")
    
    # Scan for public buckets
    public_buckets = scan_public_buckets()
    
    if not public_buckets:
        logger.info("No public buckets found. Organization is secure.")
        return
    
    # Process each public bucket
    for bucket_info in public_buckets:
        # Create SCC finding
        create_scc_finding(bucket_info)
        
        # Attempt remediation (set auto_remediate=True for automatic fix)
        remediate_public_bucket(bucket_info, auto_remediate=False)
        
        # Enable additional security features
        enable_bucket_security_features(bucket_info['bucket_name'])
    
    logger.info("Scan and remediation complete")

if __name__ == "__main__":
    import time
    main()

nist_csf: RS.MI-3 - Newly identified vulnerabilities are mitigated
validation_steps: Enable Security Command Center: gcloud services enable securitycenter.googleapis.com
Run scan script and verify public buckets are detected
Check SCC dashboard for findings: https://console.cloud.google.com/security/command-center
Test auto-remediation on non-production bucket
Set up Cloud Function to run scanner on schedule (daily recommended)

mitigations.title: Implement VPC Service Controls for Data Exfiltration Prevention
mitigations.description: Create security perimeters around GCS buckets using VPC Service Controls to prevent data exfiltration even if IAM is misconfigured. This provides defense-in-depth by restricting data access to specific VPCs and IP ranges.

code_example.code:
#!/bin/bash
# VPC Service Controls Setup for GCS Buckets

set -euo pipefail

# Variables
ORG_ID="YOUR_ORG_ID"
PROJECT_ID="YOUR_PROJECT_ID"
PROJECT_NUMBER="YOUR_PROJECT_NUMBER"
ACCESS_POLICY_TITLE="storage-security-perimeter"
PERIMETER_NAME="storage_perimeter"

# Colors for output
RED='\033[0;31m'
GREEN='\033[0;32m'
YELLOW='\033[1;33m'
NC='\033[0m' # No Color

echo -e "${GREEN}Setting up VPC Service Controls for GCS...${NC}"

# 1. Enable required APIs
echo -e "${YELLOW}Enabling required APIs...${NC}"
gcloud services enable accesscontextmanager.googleapis.com \
  cloudresourcemanager.googleapis.com \
  storage-api.googleapis.com \
  --project=${PROJECT_ID}

# 2. Create Access Policy (if not exists)
echo -e "${YELLOW}Creating Access Policy...${NC}"
ACCESS_POLICY_NAME=$(gcloud access-context-manager policies list \
  --organization=${ORG_ID} \
  --format="value(name)" \
  --filter="title:${ACCESS_POLICY_TITLE}" || echo "")

if [ -z "${ACCESS_POLICY_NAME}" ]; then
  ACCESS_POLICY_NAME=$(gcloud access-context-manager policies create \
    --organization=${ORG_ID} \
    --title=${ACCESS_POLICY_TITLE} \
    --format="value(name)")
  echo -e "${GREEN}Created Access Policy: ${ACCESS_POLICY_NAME}${NC}"
else
  echo -e "${GREEN}Using existing Access Policy: ${ACCESS_POLICY_NAME}${NC}"
fi

# 3. Define Access Level (trusted IPs and device policy)
echo -e "${YELLOW}Creating Access Level with IP restrictions...${NC}"
cat > /tmp/access-level.yaml <<EOF
name: ${ACCESS_POLICY_NAME}/accessLevels/corporate_network
title: Corporate Network Access
basic:
  combiningFunction: AND
  conditions:
  - ipSubnetworks:
    - '203.0.113.0/24'  # Corporate office IP range
    - '198.51.100.0/24' # VPN IP range
    requireScreenLock: true
    requireCorpOwned: true
  - devicePolicy:
      allowedDeviceManagementLevels:
      - COMPLETE
      - SECURE
      requireAdminApproval: false
      requireScreenlock: true
  - members:
    - 'serviceAccount:app-service@${PROJECT_ID}.iam.gserviceaccount.com'
EOF

gcloud access-context-manager levels create corporate_network \
  --policy=${ACCESS_POLICY_NAME} \
  --title="Corporate Network Access" \
  --basic-level-spec=/tmp/access-level.yaml \
  || echo "Access level already exists"

# 4. Create Service Perimeter for GCS
echo -e "${YELLOW}Creating VPC Service Perimeter...${NC}"
cat > /tmp/perimeter.yaml <<EOF
name: ${ACCESS_POLICY_NAME}/servicePerimeters/${PERIMETER_NAME}
title: GCS Storage Perimeter
status:
  resources:
  - projects/${PROJECT_NUMBER}
  restrictedServices:
  - storage.googleapis.com
  - storage-api.googleapis.com
  - storage-component.googleapis.com
  vpcAccessibleServices:
    enableRestriction: true
    allowedServices:
    - storage.googleapis.com
    - storage-api.googleapis.com
  accessLevels:
  - ${ACCESS_POLICY_NAME}/accessLevels/corporate_network
  egressPolicies:
  - egressFrom:
      identityType: ANY_IDENTITY
    egressTo:
      resources:
      - projects/${PROJECT_NUMBER}
      operations:
      - serviceName: storage.googleapis.com
        methodSelectors:
        - method: google.storage.objects.get
        - method: google.storage.objects.list
  ingressPolicies:
  - ingressFrom:
      identityType: ANY_SERVICE_ACCOUNT
      sources:
      - accessLevel: ${ACCESS_POLICY_NAME}/accessLevels/corporate_network
    ingressTo:
      resources:
      - projects/${PROJECT_NUMBER}
      operations:
      - serviceName: storage.googleapis.com
        methodSelectors:
        - method: '*'
EOF

gcloud access-context-manager perimeters create ${PERIMETER_NAME} \
  --policy=${ACCESS_POLICY_NAME} \
  --title="GCS Storage Perimeter" \
  --resources=projects/${PROJECT_NUMBER} \
  --restricted-services=storage.googleapis.com,storage-api.googleapis.com \
  --access-levels=${ACCESS_POLICY_NAME}/accessLevels/corporate_network \
  --enable-vpc-accessible-services \
  --vpc-allowed-services=storage.googleapis.com,storage-api.googleapis.com \
  || echo "Perimeter already exists"

# 5. Configure perimeter bridge for authorized cross-project access
echo -e "${YELLOW}Creating perimeter bridge for authorized projects...${NC}"
gcloud access-context-manager perimeters create bridge_to_logging \
  --policy=${ACCESS_POLICY_NAME} \
  --perimeter-type=bridge \
  --perimeter-resources=projects/${PROJECT_NUMBER},projects/LOGGING_PROJECT_NUMBER \
  || echo "Bridge already exists"

# 6. Test perimeter (this should fail from unauthorized location)
echo -e "${YELLOW}Testing VPC Service Controls...${NC}"
echo "Attempting to access bucket from current location..."
gsutil ls gs://test-bucket-${PROJECT_ID} || {
  echo -e "${RED}Access blocked by VPC Service Controls (expected if outside perimeter)${NC}"
}

# 7. Enable dry-run mode for testing (optional)
echo -e "${YELLOW}Enabling dry-run mode for testing...${NC}"
gcloud access-context-manager perimeters update ${PERIMETER_NAME} \
  --policy=${ACCESS_POLICY_NAME} \
  --set-enforced-mode=dryrun

echo -e "${GREEN}VPC Service Controls setup complete!${NC}"
echo ""
echo "Next steps:"
echo "1. Review VPC-SC logs: gcloud logging read 'protoPayload.metadata.\"@type\"=\"type.googleapis.com/google.cloud.audit.VpcServiceControlAuditMetadata\"'"
echo "2. Test access from inside perimeter (corporate network or authorized service account)"
echo "3. Switch to enforced mode: gcloud access-context-manager perimeters update ${PERIMETER_NAME} --set-enforced-mode=enforced"
echo "4. Monitor violations in Security Command Center"

iso27001_controls: A.9.1.2 - Access to networks and network services
nist_csf: PR.PT-4 - Communications and control networks are protected
validation_steps: Verify perimeter creation: gcloud access-context-manager perimeters list --policy=POLICY_NAME
Test access from outside perimeter (should fail)
Test access from inside perimeter or via authorized service account (should succeed)
Review VPC-SC audit logs for access attempts
Verify dry-run violations before enforcing: gcloud logging read 'protoPayload.metadata.dryRun=true'

indicators: Unexpected data egress from GCS buckets to unknown IP addresses
High volume of storage.objects.list or storage.objects.get API calls from public IPs
IAM policy changes adding 'allUsers' or 'allAuthenticatedUsers' members
Bucket enumeration attempts using predictable naming patterns
Signed URLs accessed from anomalous geographic locations
Large file downloads outside business hours
Service account key creation followed by immediate bucket access

log_queries.query:
# Detect public IAM binding changes on GCS buckets
resource.type="gcs_bucket"
protoPayload.methodName="storage.setIamPermissions"
protoPayload.serviceData.policyDelta.bindingDeltas.member=("allUsers" OR "allAuthenticatedUsers")
protoPayload.serviceData.policyDelta.bindingDeltas.action="ADD"
| fields timestamp, resource.labels.bucket_name, protoPayload.authenticationInfo.principalEmail, protoPayload.serviceData.policyDelta.bindingDeltas

// Detect GCP bucket access from Azure Sentinel via GCP logs export
GCPAuditLogs
| where ResourceType == "gcs_bucket"
| where MethodName in ("storage.objects.get", "storage.objects.list")
| where PrincipalEmail == "allUsers@" or PrincipalEmail == "allAuthenticatedUsers@"
| summarize AccessCount = count(), UniqueIPs = dcount(CallerIP) by BucketName, TimeGenerated
| where AccessCount > 100 or UniqueIPs > 50
| order by AccessCount desc

index=gcp_audit sourcetype=gcp:cloudaudit:activity
resource.type="gcs_bucket"
protoPayload.methodName IN ("storage.objects.get", "storage.objects.list", "storage.setIamPermissions")
| eval is_public=if(match(protoPayload.request.policy.bindings{}.members{}, "allUsers|allAuthenticatedUsers"), 1, 0)
| where is_public=1
| stats count by resource.labels.bucket_name, protoPayload.authenticationInfo.principalEmail, protoPayload.requestMetadata.callerIp
| where count > 50
| sort -count

event.provider: "storage.googleapis.com" AND event.action: ("storage.objects.get" OR "storage.objects.list" OR "storage.setIamPermissions") AND (gcp.audit.authentication_info.principal_email: "allUsers" OR gcp.audit.authentication_info.principal_email: "allAuthenticatedUsers") AND event.outcome: "success"

log_queries.siem: Amazon CloudWatch Insights - GCP logs via cross-cloud SIEM
log_queries.query:
fields @timestamp, resource.labels.bucket_name, protoPayload.authenticationInfo.principalEmail, protoPayload.requestMetadata.callerIp
| filter protoPayload.serviceName = "storage.googleapis.com"
| filter protoPayload.methodName in ["storage.objects.get", "storage.objects.list"]
| filter protoPayload.authenticationInfo.principalEmail in ["allUsers", "allAuthenticatedUsers"]
| stats count(*) as AccessCount by resource.labels.bucket_name, protoPayload.requestMetadata.callerIp
| filter AccessCount > 100
| sort AccessCount desc
